title: SCF execute regions are improperly inlined into parent modules
body:
I have just come across the following `scf.execute_region` operation that is inlined into the parent module in a somewhat unsavory way:
```mlir
hw.module @Foo() {
  %c0_i5 = hw.constant 0 : i5
  %c-118_i8 = hw.constant -118 : i8
  %0 = llhd.constant_time <0ns, 0d, 1e>
  %c0_i153 = hw.constant 0 : i153
  %a = llhd.sig %c0_i153 : i153
  scf.execute_region {
    cf.br ^bb1
  ^bb1:  // pred: ^bb0
    llhd.drv %a, %c0_i153 after %0 : !hw.inout<i153>
    %1 = llhd.sig.extract %a from %c-118_i8 : (!hw.inout<i153>) -> !hw.inout<i5>
    llhd.drv %1, %c0_i5 after %0 : !hw.inout<i5>
    scf.yield
  }
  hw.output
}
```
Running this through `circt-opt --canonicalize` yields the following:
```mlir
hw.module @Foo() {
  %c0_i5 = hw.constant 0 : i5
  %c-118_i8 = hw.constant -118 : i8
  %0 = llhd.constant_time <0ns, 0d, 1e>
  %c0_i153 = hw.constant 0 : i153
  %a = llhd.sig %c0_i153 : i153
  // ----- 8< -----
  llhd.drv %a, %c0_i153 after %0 : !hw.inout<i153>
  %1 = llhd.sig.extract %a from %c-118_i8 : (!hw.inout<i153>) -> !hw.inout<i5>
  llhd.drv %1, %c0_i5 after %0 : !hw.inout<i5>
  // ----- 8< -----
  hw.output
}
```
I'm not quite sure what to make of this. The `llhd.drv` in this case have store-like side effects, and the second drive overwrites part of the value written by the first drive. This is valid in an SSACFG region like `scf.execute_region`, but inlining this into a graph region is problematic. Within a module the drives co-exist and actually produce a drive conflict on the bits of `%a` that are targeted by both drives.

@maerhart Do you have any ideas on how we could deal with something like this?
code_blocks:
hw.module @Foo() {
  %c0_i5 = hw.constant 0 : i5
  %c-118_i8 = hw.constant -118 : i8
  %0 = llhd.constant_time <0ns, 0d, 1e>
  %c0_i153 = hw.constant 0 : i153
  %a = llhd.sig %c0_i153 : i153
  scf.execute_region {
    cf.br ^bb1
  ^bb1:  // pred: ^bb0
    llhd.drv %a, %c0_i153 after %0 : !hw.inout<i153>
    %1 = llhd.sig.extract %a from %c-118_i8 : (!hw.inout<i153>) -> !hw.inout<i5>
    llhd.drv %1, %c0_i5 after %0 : !hw.inout<i5>
    scf.yield
  }
  hw.output
}
hw.module @Foo() {
  %c0_i5 = hw.constant 0 : i5
  %c-118_i8 = hw.constant -118 : i8
  %0 = llhd.constant_time <0ns, 0d, 1e>
  %c0_i153 = hw.constant 0 : i153
  %a = llhd.sig %c0_i153 : i153
  // ----- 8< -----
  llhd.drv %a, %c0_i153 after %0 : !hw.inout<i153>
  %1 = llhd.sig.extract %a from %c-118_i8 : (!hw.inout<i153>) -> !hw.inout<i5>
  llhd.drv %1, %c0_i5 after %0 : !hw.inout<i5>
  // ----- 8< -----
  hw.output
}
